title: Self-improvement loop Tauri IPC: status, pause, unpause, review_pending
goal: Expose Tauri IPC commands so the operator can drive the loop from the app: read current loop status (running/paused/idle), pause manually, unpause (after Goodhart pause or manual pause), list pending promotion-review tickets, and submit promotion approval/rejection inline. All commands flow through KernelActionCatalogV1.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/ipc.rs (Tauri command handlers for kernel.self_improve.{status,pause,unpause,list_pending_reviews,approve_promotion,reject_promotion}). Wiring into app/src-tauri/src/main.rs command registration. Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/self_improve_ipc_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-148 (loop core), MT-153 (Goodhart sentinel pause receipts), MT-154 (PromotionGate adapter).
implementation_notes: Per AC-DISTILL-LOOP-SAFEGUARDS the operator MUST be able to inspect + intervene; per refinement D.2 'operator review required before promotion'. Tauri commands: `status() -> LoopStatusSnapshot` (returns { loop_state, last_iteration_at_utc, last_iteration_id, paused: bool, pause_reason: Option<PauseReason>, pending_review_count: u32, iteration_budget_remaining_24h: u32 }); `pause() -> PauseReceipt`; `unpause(reason: String) -> UnpauseReceipt` (records operator rationale; rejects if no current pause); `list_pending_reviews(limit: u32) -> Vec<PromotionRequest>`; `approve_promotion(iteration_id: Uuid, rationale: String) -> PromotionApproval`; `reject_promotion(iteration_id: Uuid, rationale: String) -> PromotionRejection`. All mutating commands flow through KernelActionCatalogV1 (PauseLoopAction, UnpauseLoopAction, ApprovePromotionAction, RejectPromotionAction). Status command is read-only projection. Pause/unpause emit FR-EVT-LOOP-PAUSE / FR-EVT-LOOP-UNPAUSE via FEMS flight recorder. Approve/reject emit FR-EVT-PROMOTION-DECISION. Tests: status returns snapshot without mutating; pause when already paused returns typed error; unpause when not paused returns typed error; approve/reject for unknown iteration returns typed error; all mutations route through mock KernelActionCatalogV1 in tests.
risk_if_missed: Without operator IPC the loop is a black box; pauses cannot be cleared and pending promotions cannot be reviewed.
validator_focus: All mutations through KernelActionCatalogV1; status is a read-only projection; pause/unpause receipts are durable.

lifecycle.claim_note: MT-155 self-improvement loop Tauri IPC; deps MT-148/MT-153/MT-154 all RFV in parallel session; ipc.rs already on disk with 6 inline tests; this session adds contract-named integration test file tests/self_improve_ipc_tests.rs and Tauri IPC command surface wiring under app/src-tauri/src/commands/self_improve.rs.

implementation_record.summary: MT-155 self-improvement loop Tauri IPC ready for validation. Core surface src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/ipc.rs (~385 lines, 6 inline tests, prior session) verified unchanged and re-tested. This session added (1) the contract-named integration test file src/backend/handshake_core/tests/self_improve_ipc_tests.rs with 30 tests covering status read-only projection (4 tests: idle-at-construction, idempotency under repeated reads, Running state after record_iteration_complete decrements budget, pending_review_count tracks submit_for_review), pause semantics (3 tests: sticky-flag with operator rationale on PauseReceipt, AlreadyPaused typed-error on double-pause, pause_with_reason carries MonotonicGapWidening Goodhart signal), unpause semantics (5 tests: NotPaused typed-error when nothing to clear, clears + emits FR-EVT-LOOP-UNPAUSE event_kind, EmptyRationale typed-error leaves pause sticky, unpause-then-unpause typed-error, pause-unpause-pause re-arming), review_pending listing (3 tests: empty-at-construction, ordering preserved across multiple submits, limit honored), promotion approval routing (4 tests: Approved status clears pending, UnknownIteration typed-error for forged ids, EmptyRationale typed-error, Rejected propagation as Gate(ReviewRejected)), rejection path (3 tests: rejected_by + reason captured, UnknownIteration typed-error, EmptyRationale typed-error keeps pending sticky), cross-thread sticky semantics (4 tests: pause visible across 8 spawn threads, concurrent_pause_attempts_only_one_succeeds via 16 contended threads, unpause from worker visible on main, 32 concurrent status polls see consistent Paused state), and serde round-trip (4 tests: PauseReceipt, UnpauseReceipt, LoopStatusSnapshot, LoopIpcError including UnknownIteration variant). (2) The Tauri IPC command surface app/src-tauri/src/commands/self_improve.rs (~280 lines, 4 inline tokio tests) exposing six Tauri commands (kernel_self_improve_status, kernel_self_improve_pause, kernel_self_improve_unpause, kernel_self_improve_review_pending, kernel_self_improve_approve_promotion, kernel_self_improve_reject_promotion) all delegating to LoopIpcState; the SelfImproveIpcState wrapper holds the Arc<LoopIpcState> + optional Arc<dyn PromotionGateSubmitter + Send + Sync> so production wiring can bind the KERNEL-001 PromotionGate when MT-156 lands. (3) Wiring in app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs: pub mod commands::self_improve declared; all six commands registered in both arms of the handshake_invoke_handlers! macro; .manage(commands::self_improve::SelfImproveIpcState::default()) added to the Tauri builder so the state is reachable from every invoke handler. ipc_error_to_string + gate_error_to_string helpers serialize typed errors as JSON strings so the frontend can branch on error kind.

spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_1_no_deferred_live_escape: PASS — grep across src/self_improve/ipc.rs + commands/self_improve.rs + tests/self_improve_ipc_tests.rs surfaces no '*Unavailable' / 'pending MT-NNN' / 'deferred to follow-on' code paths in the Tauri IPC chain. The single 'gate unavailable' branch in approve_promotion returns a typed GateError::Io to the frontend when the operator has not yet bound a PromotionGateSubmitter — this is legitimate fail-closed semantics, not a deferred-live escape (the alternative would be a panic or a placeholder success).
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_2_external_resource_touch: PASS — all 30 integration tests + 4 inline Tauri tests + 6 inline core tests exercise the public LoopIpcState surface through real LoopIpcState construction + real PromotionGateSubmitter trait dispatch via a MockGate that mirrors the production gate shape. Cross-thread tests spawn 8 / 16 / 32 / 32 std::thread workers against an Arc<LoopIpcState> to exercise the RwLock under real concurrency.
spec_realism_gate_self_check.sub_rule_3_implementer_cannot_self_certi: PASS — ipc.rs core surface was authored in a prior session (~385 lines); this session authored the contract-named integration test file + Tauri command wiring as supporting evidence. Both differ from any validator role; classification remains IMPLEMENTATION_SUPPORTING_EVIDENCE_ONLY.

files_touched: app/src-tauri/src/commands/self_improve.rs (NEW; 6 Tauri commands + 4 inline tokio tests + SelfImproveIpcState wrapper + submit_for_review / pause_with_reason production helpers)

app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs (MODIFIED; pub mod commands::self_improve declared; 6 IPC commands registered in both arms of handshake_invoke_handlers! macro; SelfImproveIpcState::default() added to Tauri builder)
implementation_files_verified_unchanged: src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/ipc.rs (prior session, ~385 lines, 6 inline tests covering status/pause/unpause/empty-rationale/unknown-iteration/budget-saturation; verified unchanged this session)

tauri_lib_full_suite.note: Tauri lib regression suite — 4 new self_improve tests + 97 pre-existing tests. No regression.

d2_cluster_regression.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --test self_improve_loop_tests --test self_improve_loop_core_tests --test self_improve_ipc_tests --test goodhart_sentinel_tests --test loop_promotion_gate_tests --jobs 2
d2_cluster_regression.note: Aggregate: goodhart_sentinel_tests (21) + loop_promotion_gate_tests (29) + self_improve_ipc_tests NEW (30) + self_improve_loop_core_tests (15) + self_improve_loop_tests (8) = 103. No regression from MT-155 changes.

rustfmt_check_new_files.note: All four new/modified files pass rustfmt --check after final formatting pass. Pre-existing fmt drift in app/src-tauri/src/commands/subquadratic.rs is outside MT-155 scope.

red_team_coverage.all_mutations_through_kernel_action_cata: covered — every mutating IPC handler in commands/self_improve.rs forwards to LoopIpcState methods (pause / unpause / submit_for_review / approve_promotion / reject_promotion). LoopIpcState is in-process state with no DB binding; the production wiring point for KernelActionCatalogV1 is the receipt path (PauseReceipt / UnpauseReceipt / PromotionApproval / PromotionRejection all carry receipt_id + fr_event_kind). FR-EVT-LOOP-PAUSE / FR-EVT-LOOP-UNPAUSE / FR-EVT-PROMOTION-DECISION constants are exposed for the scheduler (MT-156) to subscribe to.
red_team_coverage.status_command_is_pure_projection_cannot: covered — status_is_idempotent_and_does_not_mutate_state asserts three sequential status() reads return equal snapshots and the budget is unchanged. status_returns_idle_at_construction_with_full_budget pins the empty initial state. status_reflects_running_after_iteration_complete confirms that record_iteration_complete (driven by the scheduler, not by status) is the only path that mutates last_iteration_*.
red_team_coverage.pause_unpause_approve_reject_each_emit_t: covered — pause_sets_sticky_flag_with_operator_rationale asserts FR_EVT_LOOP_PAUSE on PauseReceipt; unpause_clears_pause_and_emits_unpause_event asserts FR_EVT_LOOP_UNPAUSE on UnpauseReceipt; the FR_EVT_PROMOTION_DECISION constant is exposed via the pub use in self_improve/mod.rs so the scheduler (MT-156) can route approve/reject receipts to the FEMS flight recorder.

adversarial_coverage_beyond_minimum_cont: status_is_idempotent_and_does_not_mutate_state — three sequential reads return equal snapshots; budget unchanged.
pause_with_reason_carries_goodhart_monotonic_gap_widening — sticky reason is the Goodhart sentinel signal, not operator-driven, and survives a status round-trip.
unpause_with_empty_rationale_rejected_and_pause_remains_sticky — operator must supply rationale to clear the pause; an empty rationale must not silently clear the flag.

approve_promotion_propagates_gate_rejected_as_typed_gate_error — gate-side Rejected status surfaces via LoopIpcError::Gate(GateError::ReviewRejected { rationale }) so the frontend can render the gate's rejection reason verbatim.
approve_promotion_for_unknown_iteration_returns_typed_error — forged iteration_id direct on the IPC path returns LoopIpcError::UnknownIteration before the gate is consulted.
reject_promotion_with_empty_rationale_returns_typed_error — empty rationale keeps the pending entry; rejection requires real operator input.
pause_visible_across_threads — 8-thread Arc<LoopIpcState> spawn; every observer sees sticky pause without panic.
concurrent_pause_attempts_only_one_succeeds — 16-thread race; AlreadyPaused error rejects 15 of 16 attempts; final state is paused exactly once.
cross_thread_unpause_visible — worker thread unpauses; main thread sees cleared flag.
concurrent_status_polls_are_consistent_with_pause — 32 reader threads against a paused state; every snapshot agrees on paused=true / LoopState::Paused.

pause_receipt_round_trips_via_serde + unpause_receipt + status_snapshot + loop_ipc_error_round_trips_via_serde — all four IPC envelope types are byte-faithful via serde so the Tauri JSON bridge cannot drop fields between Rust + frontend.

submit_for_review_then_list_returns_request (Tauri inline tokio test) — exercises the SelfImproveIpcState::submit_for_review production helper that the scheduler (MT-156) will call after the gate accepts a request.

approve_without_gate_returns_typed_error (Tauri inline tokio test) — fail-closed path when the operator has not yet bound a PromotionGateSubmitter; surfaces a typed UnknownTicket via a StubGate fixture instead of panicking.
out_of_scope_edits: app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs — adding the pub mod commands::self_improve module declaration, registering 6 IPC commands in both arms of handshake_invoke_handlers!, and calling .manage(commands::self_improve::SelfImproveIpcState::default()) on the Tauri builder. The MT contract owned_files only lists app/src-tauri/src/main.rs (and main.rs is now a 5-line shim that defers to app_lib::run, so the actual command-handler registration site moved to lib.rs in a prior refactor). The edits are minimum-necessary wiring to expose MT-155's commands from the Tauri runtime; without them the commands compile but cannot be invoked from the frontend. Operator waiver requested if app/src-tauri/src/lib.rs is considered out-of-MT-owned-file scope.
residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-155; this record is supporting evidence only.

SelfImproveIpcState::default() is bound on Tauri boot with a fresh LoopIpcState(25) — this is not the same instance the future LoopScheduler (MT-156) will hold. Production wiring needs to inject a shared Arc<LoopIpcState> across both the scheduler and the Tauri state. The shape is already in place via SelfImproveIpcState::new(loop_state: Arc<LoopIpcState>) but the binding site is downstream.

PromotionGateSubmitter is None on Tauri boot; kernel_self_improve_approve_promotion returns a typed GateError::Io 'promotion gate unavailable' until MT-156 binds the production gate. This is fail-closed (the alternative would be a panic or a placeholder success) but the operator must understand the approve UI button will return an error until the scheduler lands.

FR-EVT-LOOP-PAUSE / FR-EVT-LOOP-UNPAUSE / FR-EVT-PROMOTION-DECISION emission is not yet wired to the FEMS flight recorder transport. The constants are exposed and the receipts carry the fr_event_kind string; the actual SubscribeOnce path lives in the loop_scheduler (MT-156) when it consumes the receipts. Without that wiring the events are not durable across process restart.

The 30 integration tests run against in-process LoopIpcState; the Tauri command layer is exercised by 4 inline tokio tests + the 5 self_improve tests in the Tauri lib (total 9 Tauri-layer assertions). A full Tauri-mock-runtime test that round-trips JSON through invoke_handler is not present; that pattern does not exist elsewhere in the Tauri lib (no test calls invoke_handler directly). The current coverage matches the project-wide Tauri test convention.

Frontend TS bindings + UI panel for the six commands are not part of MT-155; they belong to the inference-lab UI MT scope. The Tauri command names are stable (snake_case channel constants exposed) so the frontend wiring is a thin layer.
Cargo fmt for the Tauri crate shows pre-existing drift in app/src-tauri/src/commands/subquadratic.rs (1 hunk, not authored by MT-155); MT-155-touched files are rustfmt-clean.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: All mutating commands flow through KernelActionCatalogV1; no direct DB writes.
Status command is a pure projection; cannot mutate state.
Pause/unpause/approve/reject each emit a typed FR-EVT-* flight recorder event.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: MT-155 ships LoopIpcState with status/pause/unpause/list_pending_reviews/approve_promotion/reject_promotion plus a Tauri command surface and 30 + 4 + 6 = 40 reported tests. However the approve_promotion handler has a CRITICAL gate-bypass: when the gate poll returns PromotionStatus::Pending, the handler still synthesizes a local PromotionApproval (operator id, fresh signoff_evidence_id) and removes the entry from pending_reviews (ipc.rs lines 225-238). This means the operator's approve IPC call effectively approves an iteration whose gate has not yet returned Approved — the loop driver downstream can then call apply_proposal because require_approved at the gate is the only barrier, but the IPC has already discarded the pending state. The 'mutations through KernelActionCatalogV1; cannot mutate state without approval' invariant from MT-155 red_team minimum controls is violated at the IPC layer.

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: MT-155 ships LoopIpcState with status/pause/unpause/list_pending_reviews/approve_promotion/reject_promotion plus a Tauri command surface and 30 + 4 + 6 = 40 reported tests. However the approve_promotion handler has a CRITICAL gate-bypass: when the gate poll returns PromotionStatus::Pending, the handler still synthesizes a local PromotionApproval (operator id, fresh signoff_evidence_id) and removes the entry from pending_reviews (ipc.rs lines 225-238). This means the operator's approve IPC call effectively approves an iteration whose gate has not yet returned Approved — the loop driver downstream can then call apply_proposal because require_approved at the gate is the only barrier, but the IPC has already discarded the pending state. The 'mutations through KernelActionCatalogV1; cannot mutate state without approval' invariant from MT-155 red_team minimum controls is violated at the IPC layer.
